data.title: Obsidian Energy announces $175M private placement of senior notes
data.description:
• Obsidian Energy (OBE:CA) entered an underwriting agreement to privately place $175M of 8.125% senior unsecured notes due December 3, 2030.
  • Closing is expected around December 3, 2025.
  • Net proceeds will redeem $80.8M of existing 11.95% notes due 2027, reduce borrowings under the $235M credit facility to about $5M, and cover transaction expenses.
  • The company has issued a conditional redemption notice for the 2027 notes pending successful completion of the new offering.

data.title: Regeneron gets FDA nod for Eylea in macular edema treatment
data.description:
• Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) has received approval from the U.S. FDA for Eylea HD (aflibercept) Injection 8 mg for patients suffering from macular edema due to retinal vein occlusion. 
  • Additionally, the FDA has  authorized a monthly dosing option every 4 weeks for certain patients who may find this schedule beneficial across various approved uses.

data.title: Xerox appoints new CFO
data.description:
• Xerox (XRX) named Chuck Butler as its new chief financial officer, effective December 3, 2025.
  • Current CFO Mirlanda Gecaj will depart the company on December 2 by mutual decision.
  • Butler previously served as CFO at Lexmark and helped guide its July 2025 acquisition by Xerox.

data.title: Cloudflare trades in red for seven straight sessions
data.description:
Cloudflare (NET) continued losses for seven straight sessions as the stock closed 0.17% lower at $196.20 on Wednesday.

The internet security company lost nearly 16.35% in the last six trading sessions. NET is down nearly 7% over the past one month.

The stock fell 2.83% to $196.53 on Tuesday as a major outage at the company disrupted major apps, including X, ChatGPT and others.

As per Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, NET has a Hold rating with a score of 3.39 out of 5. The company received A+ for revisions, A- for momentum and B+ for growth and B- for profitability, but an F grade in valuation dragged down the rating.

Seeking Alpha analyst Amrita Roy also rated the stock as Hold, saying that Cloudflare delivered strong Q3 FY25 results, with accelerated revenue growth, robust $100K+ customer momentum, and raised full-year guidance.

 “Despite operational excellence, Cloudflare's high valuation has led it to underperform against other high-growth cybersecurity names, like CrowdStrike and Zscaler, over a 3M period,” Roy highlighted.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 19 out of 34 analysts assigned NET a Buy and above rating, and 13 recommended it as Hold, while one rated as Sell and one as Strong Sell.

Cloudflare's shares have gained nearly 80% so far this year, outperforming the broader benchmark index, which has posted a 12.5% gain during the same period.

data.title: Universal Technical Institute FQ4 revenue grows but profits stunted by higher operating costs
data.description:
Universal Technical Institute (UTI) reported better-than-expected results for the fiscal fourth quarter, bolstered by a 13% increase in revenue and 8.1% increase in the number of full-time students.

The technical school’s profits, however, were unchanged from a year ago, weighed down by increased operating expenses, and expected to decline by 33% for FY26 to a range of $0.71 to $0.80 per share, as much as $0.23 less than anticipated.

Shares are up more than 4% in volatile after-hours trading.

“Fiscal 2025 was an exceptional year for Universal Technical Institute and a defining start to the second phase of our North Star strategy," said CEO Jerome Grant. "We exceeded every major operational target we set and even surpassed our twice-raised revenue guidance range with 14% year-over-year growth. Entering this next chapter, we are executing from the strongest position in our company's history, and these results reinforce the durability of our model and prove that our platform can scale efficiently while maintaining our high quality.”

For the fiscal fourth quarter, the company earned a profit of $0.34 per share, unchanged from last year and 8 cents better than expected, on a 13.2% increase in sales to $222.4M, $3M above estimates.

For FY26, UTI expects enrollment to increase by 8% year-over-year, driving a 9% increase in revenue to a range of $905M and $915M versus $902M estimates.

data.title: ProPhase Labs signals $50M net collection target and strategic initiatives as Crown Medical litigation advances
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ProPhase Labs (PRPH) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Ted Karkus, Chairman & CEO, opened by emphasizing the critical importance of shareholder voting on the company's proxy, noting, "If you do not vote for our proxy, you are putting our company in harm's way."
  • Karkus announced that ProPhase is pursuing several strategic initiatives, including a potential reverse stock split and a crypto treasury strategy, but stated, "I will not do anything that does not significantly recognize the underlying value and assets of our company and is accretive for our shareholders."
  • The appointment of Crown Medical as special counsel to pursue approximately $150 million in uncollected COVID testing receivables from about 1,000 insurance companies was highlighted as a major milestone, with Karkus stating, "Crown is now going after that" and "They absolutely believe we're going to collect at least $50 million net."
  • Karkus described operational progress across subsidiaries: the BE-Smart esophageal cancer test, with recent publication in a major journal and Mayo Clinic partnership; Nebula Genomics’ transition to a subscription model and cost reductions; and ongoing consideration of dietary supplement expansion contingent on cash flow.
  • The CEO reiterated that, pending successful collections, priorities are to pay off debt, consider stock buybacks, and fund core growth areas.

Outlook

  • Management maintains the crypto treasury strategy as a potential option but not a primary objective, influenced by market conditions and anticipated cash influx from Crown Medical collections.
  • Karkus indicated work on another, undisclosed strategic initiative that could create significant shareholder value, contingent on proxy approval and maintaining NASDAQ compliance.
  • The company expects settlements from insurance companies to begin in the coming months, which would "change our financial structure pretty quickly."

Financial Results

  • Karkus reported a current market cap of approximately $12 million, with 46.1 million shares outstanding and a share price around $0.25 to $0.30.
  • One small settlement from Crown Medical collections has been received, and further settlements are anticipated to start ramping up in the near term.
  • The company continues to operate with similar cash levels as recent quarters, using debt and potential equity as interim funding ahead of expected collections.
  • Significant reductions in overhead and operating expenses have been achieved, including selling the Pharmaloz manufacturing facility and shutting down the Nebula Genomics laboratory.

Q&A

  • An analyst asked about the number of major deals under negotiation. Karkus replied, "It's potentially more than 2... we have a reverse crypto strategy," and also referenced ongoing discussions related to the BE-Smart test and other company-wide deals.
  • On the impact of a potential reverse stock split, Karkus stated, "A reverse stock split in and of itself is not something to be scared of... Your percentage of share ownership doesn't change."
  • Asked about employee compensation and liquidity, Karkus explained the company has "multiple investors, large investors that want to support us with various types of funding options," and outlined plans for possible stock buybacks after collections begin.
  • Responding to concerns about delisting, Karkus clarified, "We need to vote because I need to give the other companies and bankers that I'm working with confidence that we will remain on NASDAQ."
  • When questioned on growth for the BE-Smart test, Karkus projected the asset "could be worth 10x our market cap 12 months from now, literally or less... just our esophageal cancer test alone could be worth 10x the current market cap of our company."
  • On the ongoing delay in receivable collections, Karkus explained, "We did not anticipate it would take that long to bankrupt the lab sub and get going. But now we've done it. We crossed that hurdle."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts repeatedly pressed for clarity on timelines, liquidity, and the specifics of strategic initiatives, with a tone that was generally skeptical and seeking concrete details.
  • Management’s tone during prepared remarks was confident and assertive, as Karkus stated, "I'm all in. I hope those that are listening are all in," and defended the company’s strategies and shareholder value focus.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s sentiment remained optimistic but was more insistent on shareholder action and highlighted urgency regarding proxy voting and compliance.
  • Analysts’ tone was more persistent on matters of cash flow, delisting, and operational execution than the previous quarter.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Strategic focus has shifted from a broader exploration of the crypto treasury strategy in Q2 to a more immediate emphasis on Crown Medical collections and unspecified new initiatives in Q3.
  • There is heightened urgency in Q3 about proxy voting and NASDAQ compliance as prerequisites for executing value-creating transactions.
  • The company has moved from planning for Crown Medical litigation to actual implementation, with court approval achieved and initial settlements underway.
  • Management’s confidence in the BE-Smart test remains strong, now supported by publication in a major journal and increased external interest.
  • Analysts’ questions evolved from general progress updates in Q2 to more pointed inquiries about liquidity, dilution, and timelines in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Karkus identified the company’s tight cash position as a key reason for the disconnect between market valuation and asset value, stating, "We've been tied on cash all year. That all changes... as soon as the Crown Medical starts flowing."
  • The risk of delisting due to share price was discussed, with mitigation strategies including a potential reverse stock split and the necessity of shareholder voting.
  • Management acknowledged that delays in collections were longer than anticipated due to legal complexities but now expects an accelerated process.
  • The CEO addressed concerns about dilution, emphasizing that any capital raises would be strategically timed to maximize shareholder value.

Final Takeaway

ProPhase Labs’ third quarter call underscored management’s focus on unlocking value through aggressive pursuit of Crown Medical collections, maintaining NASDAQ compliance, and advancing the BE-Smart esophageal cancer test toward commercialization. The company has taken significant steps to streamline operations and manage costs while positioning itself for potential stock buybacks and strategic partnerships once anticipated cash inflows materialize. Management reiterated confidence in the underlying assets, urging shareholders to support proxy measures critical for executing forthcoming initiatives that could reshape the company’s value proposition.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
